metadata.notes: AMK (AssetMark) excluded — delisted Nov 2024 after GTCR acquisition; also a wealth platform, not alt AM. PHL (Petershill) excluded — delisted Dec 2025. SCU (Sculptor) excluded — acquired by Rithm Nov 2023. PAG excluded — not yet publicly listed. 3i excluded — primarily balance-sheet investor, minimal third-party AUM. BIP/BNT excluded — managed vehicles within Brookfield, not independent managers. Added beyond expected list: CVC, Bridgepoint, Antin, Eurazeo, Onex, P10/Ridgepost, Vinci Compass.

universe.inclusion_rationale: Largest publicly listed alternative asset manager globally. Pure-play alternatives platform across real estate ($319B), PE ($416B), credit & insurance ($443B), and multi-asset ($96B).
Second-largest listed alt manager. Now 100% owner of Brookfield AM business after acquiring BN's 73% interest. Fee-bearing AUM over $600B.
Major pure-play alt manager; credit-dominant model with retirement services integration. 25% YoY AUM growth. On track to $1T by late 2026.
Major diversified alt manager. 17% YoY AUM growth. Perpetual capital at $321B (43% of AUM). Record $129B fundraising in 2025.
Pure-play alt manager with credit-dominant model (65% of AUM in credit). 29% YoY AUM growth. Targeting $750B by 2028.
Major global alt manager. Diversified across global PE ($163.5B), global credit ($211.3B), and AlpInvest solutions ($102B). 8% YoY AUM growth.
Diversified asset manager spanning traditional and alternative strategies. Partial-alt: majority of revenue from traditional AM; alternatives are a meaningful but minority segment.
Pure-play alt manager focused on permanent capital solutions. ~85% of management fees from permanent capital. Formed from Owl Rock + Dyal Capital (2021).
Pure-play alt manager. Diversified across PE, impact, credit, real estate, and market solutions. $303B AUM with 20%+ YoY growth.
Largest European alt platform. Pure-play alternatives across PE, infrastructure, real estate. Second-largest PE firm globally by capital raised.
Pure-play alt investment manager specializing in systematic and discretionary strategies. Record AUM with 35% YoY growth. Six consecutive years of market share gains.
Pure-play private markets firm with $220B AUM and $709B total capital responsibility. Advisory/solutions-oriented model. Key comparator for mid-scale alt managers.
Recently listed (Amsterdam) pure-play alt manager. ~€200B AUM. Fee-paying AUM of €140B. Strong deployment ($24.9B in H1 2025). Added beyond expected list.
Swiss-listed pure-play private markets firm. 32% of AUM in evergreen programs. Pioneer of private markets royalties as fifth asset class.
Pure-play private markets firm. Customized separate accounts and specialized fund solutions. 8% YoY AUM growth. Direct comparator for mid-scale alt managers.
London-listed pure-play alt manager with 30+ years of experience. Renamed from Intermediate Capital Group in 2025. Strategic partnership with Amundi for wealth distribution.
London-listed pure-play mid-market alt manager. European PE, credit, US energy infra. 20% YoY AUM growth. Added beyond expected list.
Toronto-listed multi-strategy alt manager. $50.5B third-party AUM + $8.4B proprietary capital. Majority-alt due to significant balance-sheet co-investing alongside fund management.

TARGET COMPANY. LatAm-focused pure-play alt manager. $52.6B AUM (26% YoY growth). FEAUM of $40.8B (24% YoY growth). Pro-forma FEAUM of $47.4B incl. announced acquisitions. Expanding into US GPMS capabilities.

Nasdaq-listed LatAm-focused asset manager. Formerly Vinci Partners; rebranded July 2025 after Compass merger. R$354B AUM (~$50B). Direct PAX comparator. Majority-alt due to significant public equities and advisory revenue.

NYSE-listed pure-play private markets solutions provider. Middle and lower-middle market focus. Rebranding to Ridgepost Capital (Feb 2026). Relevant size comparator for PAX. Added beyond expected list.

Paris-listed private markets manager with hybrid model (balance sheet + third-party AUM). €36.8B AUM. Third-party AUM of €27.5B growing at 10% YoY. Majority-alt due to significant proprietary balance sheet component.
Paris-listed pure-play infrastructure specialist. €33.3B AUM. Relevant peer for PAX's infrastructure franchise. Added beyond expected list.

excluded_firms.reason: Delisted November 2024 following GTCR acquisition. Also a wealth management platform (TAMP), not an alternative asset manager. Primary business is turnkey asset management for financial advisors.
Delisted from LSE as of December 5, 2025. Goldman Sachs-backed GP stakes vehicle returned $921M to minority shareholders and went private.
Acquired by Rithm Capital in November 2023 for ~$720M. No longer independently publicly traded.
Not publicly listed. Considering Hong Kong IPO (~$2B raise) but no final decision as of early 2026.
Primarily a balance-sheet principal investor. Minimal third-party AUM (~£1.7B in unlisted infra funds). Investment model is direct proprietary investing, not fee-generating fund management.
Transferred 100% of asset management business to BAM in 2025. Remaining entity is a diversified holding company. Alt-AM economics fully captured in BAM (FIRM-002). Including BN would double-count.
Insurance/annuity capital solutions entity within Brookfield ecosystem. Not an independent asset manager. Capital flows to BAM strategies.
Managed vehicle (LP) within BAM's infrastructure franchise, not an independent asset manager.
